steps.title: Understand the function notation B(4)
steps.content: In the function $B(t) = 80(2)^t$, the variable $t$ represents time in days since measurement began. When we write $B(4)$, we are evaluating the function at $t = 4$, which gives us the predicted bacteria count after 4 days.

steps.title: Interpret the value $B(4) = 1{,}280$
steps.content: The statement $B(4) = 1,280$ means: when $t = 4$ days, the function value is $1,280$ cells. In words: 4 days after the number of bacteria cells was first measured, the predicted number of bacteria cells was 1,280. That matches choice A — A is correct.
